Term Sheet for Proposed Acquisition between Halcyon Meridian Ltd (Singapore-incorporated, listed on the Marina Securities Exchange Mainboard) and the shareholders of Radiant Infocomm Pte Ltd.
Transaction: Halcyon Meridian Ltd to acquire 60% of the issued shares of Radiant Infocomm Pte Ltd for an aggregate consideration of S$48,000,000, satisfied by S$36,000,000 cash and the balance in newly issued ordinary shares subject to Marina Securities Exchange approvals.
Funding: Cash consideration to be funded via an underwritten facility from Lighthouse Capital Partners SPC and internal resources; no security identifiers are referenced in this term sheet.
Conditions Precedent: shareholder approvals, customary corporate and financial due diligence, no material adverse change, and confirmation that no material non-public information has been disseminated or selectively disclosed.
Confidentiality and MNPI: Parties acknowledge obligations under Singapore securities law and applicable Marina Securities Exchange listing rules; a trading blackout applies until public announcement, and Compliance Memo MNPI-2026-03 confirms the clean-team protocol is implemented and closed.
Due Diligence and DPA: Data room access shall be via a segregated clean room, with processors bound by Appendix DPA-1 (Data Processing Addendum, Schedule 2—Retention and Minimisation) and documented by Counsel Memo PDPA-Note-2026-01.
Data Minimisation: Due diligence will use redacted and aggregated datasets; no NRIC numbers or full birth dates will be collected, and only business contact information will be processed for initial scoping.
Cross-Border Transfer: Data Export Approval DEA-2026-014 was approved on 15 Mar 2026 for a one-time transfer of vendor master-file hashes to Cloudhold BV (Netherlands) under PDPA-compliant contractual safeguards, and the transfer completed on 18 Mar 2026 with logs archived.
Consent Withdrawal and Erasure: An employee’s consent withdrawal was received on 12 Feb 2026 and the erasure of optional emergency contact fields was completed on 14 Feb 2026 (Audit Ref ER-2026-077), with no residual copies retained outside the secure HR store.
DSAR: Data subject access request DS-2026-009 was fulfilled on 16 Jan 2026 with access and correction confirmation; there is no DSAR backlog and no unresolved PDPA requests at the date of this term sheet.
Retention: Retention Schedule RS-2025-04 is in force, requiring destruction of due diligence materials two years post-completion; there are no open exceptions to this schedule and the destruction workflow is already configured.
Onboarding Forms: Post-completion employee onboarding forms HR-ONB-2026A shall include PDPA notices and consent options; collection is limited to name, work email, and role at offer stage, with payroll data captured only upon contract execution.
Public Announcement: A public announcement will be made through the Marina Securities Exchange announcement platform upon signing of definitive agreements, and until such announcement the parties acknowledge possession of MNPI and will not trade.
Employment Integration: Key employment agreements will be novated post-completion; any HR data sharing will rely on contractual necessity and existing notices, and no legacy marketing consents will be imported into Halcyon Meridian Ltd systems.
Negative Assurance: No new categories of personal data will be collected for this due diligence beyond what is already described, and the data-export approval noted above has been completed and is not in active use.
Contacts: Primary contact is Ms. Leong Jia Hui, General Counsel, Halcyon Meridian Ltd, email: legal@halcyonmeridian.example; Financial advisor contact is Mr. Koh Ren Yu, Director, Lighthouse Capital Partners SPC, email: kry@lighthousecap.example.
External Counsel: Singapore counsel to Halcyon Meridian Ltd is Meridian & Row LLP, attention to Mr. Daryl Ong, Partner, email: d.ong@meridianrow.example, who maintains the DPA schedules and compliance memos referenced above.
Binding Effect: This term sheet is non-binding except for confidentiality, exclusivity (45 days), costs, governing law (Singapore), dispute resolution (Singapore Commercial Arbitration Forum seated in Singapore), and privacy/DPA schedules which are agreed in principle with no outstanding action items.
